Empires & Puzzles Flip Review: The Performing Seal
Is this performing seal worth your treats? We dive into Flip's unique mechanics, his bypass ability, and whether his base form can still swim in the deep end of the meta.
Flip Review: Seal of Approval or Just a Flop?
Flip: The Seal Who Brought a Swim Ring to a Gun Fight

family, released back in the sunny days of June 2023. At the time, he splashed onto the scene as a versatile healer with a unique offensive twist. He's a performing seal balancing a lifebuoy on his nose, which is frankly adorable, but in the brutal world of Empires & Puzzles, cuteness doesn't kill titans (usually). This review focuses on his
, examining if he can still tread water or if he's been left on the shore by his own costume.

Flip’s design is wholesome summer fun personified. He’s a happy seal wearing a red life vest, balancing a classic red-and-white lifebuoy. The background is a vibrant tropical beach that makes you want to sip a piña colada rather than raid a stronghold. Compared to his costume—where he inexplicably balances a giant donut and wears a uniform—the base art feels more "natural" for a beach theme, if a seal wearing a vest can be called natural. It’s lighthearted, bright, and stands out among the armies of demons and undead warlords.

Let's be real: Base Flip's stats are showing their age. With 873 Power, 834 Attack, and 867 Defense, he is significantly behind modern heroes who regularly push 1250+ Power and 1300+ stats. His HP of 1528 is particularly low by today's standards, where 2200+ is the new normal. Without Limit Breaks or emblems, he is incredibly fragile in the current meta and will likely get one-shot by modern snipers.
"To the Rescue!" is a mixed bag of utility.
45% heal is decent for an Average speed hero, but standard healers now often push 50% or add overheal (boosted health).
This is his crown jewel. Giving

allies the ability to ignore defensive buffs (including the dreaded Counterattack/Riposte) is a game-changer against passive teams relying on heroes like Ludwig, Hathor, or Obakan. It turns your tile damage into pure damage.
+50% Crit chance is a nice cherry on top, especially for Titans or heavy tile damage teams, but it relies on RNG.
Average (10 tiles). With a level 23 Mana Troop or magic/styx troops, you can get him down to 9 tiles.
45% of 1528 HP is ~687 HP. Modern healers often heal for 1000+.
200 Water damage over 2 turns. It's negligible damage in today's game—basically a mosquito bite.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Beach Party family and passives add some flavor but aren't game-breaking.
Reduces DoT damage. Situational. Nice if you're facing a burn/poison heavy team, but rarely a deciding factor.
Helpful against a very small niche of heroes (like other Beach Party heroes or select S2/S3 heroes).
As mentioned, 200 damage is tiny. It clears "Cleanse" effects sometimes, but don't count on it for kills.
Defense Up is solid. Since his base defense is low, he needs all the help he can get to survive long enough to fire.
Upon release, Flip was praised for his unique "Bypass" buff. He was a strategic counter to the heavy buff meta.
He has been power-crept hard stat-wise. His special skill effects are still unique and useful, but his survivability is a major liability.
(mostly). Unless you have absolutely no other red healer, base Flip is too weak for top-tier War or Raids. His costume is a strict upgrade in almost every way. He is still
for Titans solely for the Crit buff and Bypass if you lack other options.
The costume (Donut Vendor) is essentially Flip 2.0.
The costume brings his stats into the modern era (1141 Attack vs 834).
Cleric (Costume) vs Sorcerer (Base). Manashield (Cleric) is generally preferred for healers to resist mana control.
The costume is superior in every single metric. If you have the costume, there is almost zero reason to use the base form, except maybe for the Sorcerer class quest.
use base Flip (perhaps you don't have the costume leveled yet), survivability is key.
Maximizes his low health pool and keeps him alive to cast his buff.
His tile damage will remain mediocre.
. A dead healer provides zero buffs.
Rare ability that allows the whole team to ignore defensive buffs and counterattacks.
Increases tile damage significantly, great for Titans.
Can be too slow against fast rush teams.
His costume does everything he does, but better.
The Crit boost and Bypass are excellent for high scores.
His buffs count, and he helps punch through tanky defenses.
Can be useful against Riposte tanks (Cyprian/Boril/Obakan) if you can keep him alive.
Pair him with heroes that benefit from his Crit boost, like high tile damage heroes (e.g., Tetisheri, Khufu).
Don't rely on him as your
healer in hard content due to his low stats; bring a secondary support.
Flip + Guardian Kong + heavy hitters. Kong cleanses/reflects, Flip lets everyone bypass defenses.
Flip + Wu Kong/Ranvir/Miki + Defense Down + Elemental Defense Down.
Sabina, Melendor, or any dispeller removes his bypass/crit buffs instantly.
Heroes that prevent buffs (like various Knights or Waddles) shut him down completely.
If you pulled him early, he’s a great healer. The bypass ability will help you defeat annoying counterattack bosses in the map stages. Prioritize keeping him alive.

Base Flip is a relic. Use the costume. If you don't have the costume, he's likely a dedicated Titan hero or depth for War Cleanups against specific counterattack teams. Do not waste Alpha Aethers on the base version alone.
“SG seems to like red healers with crit chance...”
- One user noted the similarity to Phenexa, questioning the redundancy.
- A crucial clarification from the community. Flip’s bypass does
let you hit through Taunt (like Krampus or Ludwig's taunt itself), only defense

Flip is a fun, quirky hero with a mechanic that hasn't gone out of style—ignoring the enemy's defenses. While his base stats have rusted like a cheap beach chair, his spirit (and his special skill) remains useful in specific niches. If you have him, pray for the costume; if you have the costume, you have a top-tier red healer. Without it, he's a niche tool for cracking specific defensive nuts.
A nostalgic swim, but stay in the shallow end without the costume.
A comprehensive review of Flip, the 5-star Fire hero from the Beach Party family. We analyze his stats, skills, and how he holds up in the current meta compared to his costume.
